1: Mine ear hath heard and understood it.
2: I am am not inferior unto you.
3: And I desire to reason with God.
4: Ye are are all physicians of no value.
5: And it should be your wisdom.
6: And hearken to the pleadings of my lips.
9: Or as one man mocketh another, do ye so so mock him?
10: If ye do secretly accept persons.
11: And his dread fall upon you?
12: Your bodies to bodies of clay.
13: And let come on me what will will .
14: And put my life in mine hand?
15: But I will maintain mine own ways before him.
16: For an hypocrite shall not come before him.
17: And my declaration with your ears.
18: I know that I shall be justified.
19: For now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.
20: Then will I not hide myself from thee.
21: And let not thy dread make me afraid.
22: Or let me speak, and answer thou me.
23: Make me to know my transgression and my sin.
24: And holdest me for thine enemy?
25: And wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?
26: And makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.
27: Thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.
28: As a garment that is moth eaten.
